title: How to Build Unshakable Confidence and Rise Up Without Permission
guestBio: Nada Lena Nasserdeen is the Founder & CEO of Rise Up For You, author, executive coach, and former college professor. She is a global advocate for leadership, confidence, and self-worth who has helped thousands of professionals rise into their full potential from the classroom to the boardroom.
excerpt: Confidence isn't a personality trait—it's a skill you can develop. Join Aimee and executive coach Nada Nasserdeen as they explore how to overcome imposter syndrome, build unshakable confidence, and step into leadership without waiting for permission.
metaDescription: Learn how to build confidence, overcome imposter syndrome, and rise into leadership with insights from executive coach Nada Nasserdeen on The Manage Her.
keyTakeaways: Confidence is a learnable skill, not an innate personality trait that you either have or don't have.
Modern leadership requires authenticity and the courage to bridge the gap between who you are and who you're meant to be.
Overcoming imposter syndrome starts with shifting your mindset and recognizing your inherent worth and capabilities.
Rising up in leadership means stopping playing small and stepping into your purpose without waiting for permission.
Building unshakable confidence requires consistent practice and the willingness to embrace discomfort as part of growth.
Visibility and leadership challenges are shared experiences among women that can be overcome with the right strategies.
Your diverse background and experiences are assets that shape your unique vision and leadership approach.

pullQuotes.text: Confidence is a skill, not a personality trait — and leadership begins when we stop playing small and start rising into our purpose.

timestamps.label: Nada's Origin Story and Rise Up For You
Bridging the Gap Between Who You Are and Who You're Meant to Be

content:
What if I told you that the confidence you've been waiting to magically appear is actually a skill you can develop right now? In this powerful episode of The Manage Her, Aimee sits down with the incredible Nada Lena Nasserdeen, Founder & CEO of Rise Up For You, to explore exactly how you can build unshakable confidence and step into leadership without waiting for anyone's permission.

Nada's journey from college professor to executive coach to global advocate for women's leadership is a masterclass in reinvention and purpose-driven pivoting. Her diverse background has shaped her unique vision for helping thousands of professionals bridge the gap between who they are and who they're meant to be. This isn't just feel-good advice—this is strategic, actionable guidance for women ready to rise up.

The Truth About Confidence That Changes Everything

Here's what you need to hear: confidence isn't something you're born with or without. It's not a personality trait that some lucky people get while others are left wondering why they feel so uncertain. Confidence is a skill, and like any skill, it can be developed, strengthened, and mastered through intentional practice.

This reframe is revolutionary because it puts the power back in your hands. You're not waiting for confidence to strike like lightning—you're actively building it through your choices, your actions, and your willingness to step outside your comfort zone. Every time you speak up in a meeting, every time you share your ideas, every time you take on a challenge that scares you a little, you're building your confidence muscle.

Rising Up Without Permission

One of the most powerful themes Nada explores is the concept of rising up without permission. Too often, women wait for someone else to validate their readiness for leadership, to give them the green light to pursue their dreams, or to confirm that they're qualified enough to take up space. But here's the truth: no one is coming to give you permission because you already have it.

This mindset shift requires you to stop playing small and start recognizing that your voice, your perspective, and your leadership are needed right now. You don't need another certification, another year of experience, or another person's approval. You need to trust yourself and take action from where you are with what you have.

The conversation between Aimee and Nada reveals how this permission-free approach to leadership isn't just about individual success—it's about creating ripple effects that benefit entire organizations and communities. When you step into your power, you give others permission to do the same.

Conquering Imposter Syndrome Once and for All

Imposter syndrome—that nagging voice that tells you you're not qualified, that you're going to be found out, that you're somehow fooling everyone around you—is one of the biggest barriers to leadership that women face. But Nada offers a different perspective on how to handle these feelings when they arise.

Instead of trying to eliminate imposter syndrome completely, she suggests recognizing it as a sign that you're growing and challenging yourself. The goal isn't to never feel like an imposter again; it's to feel it and move forward anyway. This approach transforms imposter syndrome from a stop sign into a compass pointing toward your next area of growth.

The key is understanding that feeling unprepared or uncertain doesn't mean you actually are unprepared or unqualified. It often means you're stepping into something bigger than what you've done before, and that's exactly where growth happens. Your discomfort is data, not a reason to retreat.

Modern Leadership Requires Authentic Connection

The conversation also dives deep into what modern leadership actually looks like in today's world. Gone are the days when leadership meant having all the answers, maintaining perfect composure, or leading through intimidation and hierarchy. Today's most effective leaders are those who lead with authenticity, vulnerability, and genuine connection.

This is particularly powerful for women who have been told that emotion has no place in leadership or that they need to choose between being liked and being respected. Modern leadership embraces the full spectrum of human experience and recognizes that connecting with people on a genuine level is actually a strategic advantage, not a weakness.

Nada emphasizes that the traits many women have been conditioned to see as limitations—empathy, collaboration, intuition—are actually superpowers in today's leadership landscape. The world needs leaders who can navigate complexity with both logic and emotional intelligence, who can build bridges instead of walls, and who can inspire others through authentic connection rather than fear-based authority.

Your Diverse Background is Your Competitive Advantage

One of the most encouraging aspects of Nada's story is how her diverse background—from academia to coaching to entrepreneurship—has become her greatest asset rather than a source of confusion or scattered focus. This is a powerful message for women who worry that their non-linear career paths or varied interests make them less qualified for leadership roles.

Your diverse experiences give you a unique perspective that can't be replicated. They allow you to see connections others miss, to relate to a wider range of people, and to bring innovative solutions to complex problems. Instead of apologizing for your varied background, you can leverage it as evidence of your adaptability, curiosity, and broad skill set.

This conversation is a reminder that there's no single path to leadership and no perfect formula for success. Your journey, with all its twists and turns, has been preparing you for exactly where you are right now and exactly where you're going next.

As you listen to this episode, you'll find yourself nodding along to the shared challenges women face around visibility, self-advocacy, and stepping into leadership roles. But more importantly, you'll walk away with concrete strategies for building the unshakable confidence you need to rise up and lead without apology. The world is waiting for what you have to offer—and it's time to stop waiting for permission to share it.

quiz.title: What's Your Confidence Building Style?
quiz.description: Discover how you naturally approach building confidence and stepping into your power. Find out which strategies from episode 14 will help you rise up without permission.

questions.question: When you're facing a new challenge or opportunity that feels intimidating, what's your first instinct?

options.text: Research everything I can about it and create a detailed plan of action
Trust my gut feeling and jump in, figuring it out as I go
Talk it through with trusted friends or mentors to get their perspectives
Start with a small, manageable version of the challenge to test the waters

questions.question: How do you typically handle moments when you're doubting yourself or feeling like an imposter?

options.text: I remind myself of my qualifications and all the work I've put in to be here
I focus on why I'm passionate about this and trust that my authentic self is enough
I reach out to my support network and remember I'm not alone in feeling this way
I acknowledge the feeling and take one small confident action to shift my energy

questions.question: What gives you the strongest sense of confidence in professional settings?

options.text: Knowing I'm exceptionally well-prepared and knowledgeable about the topic
Staying true to my authentic voice and unique perspective
Feeling connected to the people around me and knowing we're in it together
Building on past successes and proven track record

questions.question: When you think about 'rising up without permission,' what resonates most with you?

options.text: Having all my ducks in a row so no one can question my readiness
Following my inner knowing even when others don't understand my vision
Creating space for other women to rise up alongside me
Taking progressively bigger steps until I'm exactly where I want to be

questions.question: How do you prefer to step outside your comfort zone?

options.text: With a clear strategy and backup plan in place
By following what feels aligned and exciting, even if it's uncertain
With encouragement and support from people who believe in me
By gradually expanding what feels comfortable over time

questions.question: What would make you feel most ready to take on a bigger leadership role?

options.text: Additional training, certifications, or expertise in key areas
A clear sense of purpose and mission that I'm passionate about
A strong team and network of supporters who can help me succeed
More experience and proven wins in progressively responsible roles

results.title: The Strategic Preparer
results.description: You build confidence through thorough preparation and mastering your craft. You believe in doing your homework and showing up ready, which is a tremendous strength. Your methodical approach means when you do step forward, you're incredibly well-equipped. The key for you is learning when 'good enough' is actually perfect, and that sometimes the best preparation is simply taking action. This episode will show you how to bridge the gap between your incredible preparation and the bold action that's waiting for you.

results.title: The Intuitive Leap-Taker
results.description: You're naturally drawn to following your instincts and taking inspired action, even when you can't see the full picture. Your willingness to trust yourself is a rare and powerful gift in leadership. You understand that confidence comes from doing, not just thinking. Your challenge might be honoring your intuitive wisdom when others question your unconventional approach. This episode will validate your natural confidence-building style and give you language to articulate why your approach works so brilliantly.

results.title: The Community Builder
results.description: You gain confidence through connection, collaboration, and lifting others as you rise. You understand that vulnerability creates strength and that authentic relationships fuel sustainable success. Your ability to see confidence as a collective experience rather than a solo journey is exactly what modern leadership needs. Sometimes you might doubt whether your collaborative approach is 'leadership enough,' but it absolutely is. This episode will reinforce why your relationship-centered confidence style is not just valid, but essential for the future of leadership.

results.title: The Gradual Expander
results.description: You build confidence by taking consistent small steps and celebrating incremental progress. You understand that sustainable growth happens through steady expansion of your comfort zone, not dramatic leaps. Your approach creates lasting confidence because it's built on a solid foundation of proven experiences. You might sometimes feel pressure to move faster or be bolder, but your methodical approach is actually incredibly wise. This episode will help you see how your steady confidence-building style is a strength, while also showing you how to recognize when you're ready for bigger steps than you think.
